w20: Dedicated CPU instance with 16 physical cores and 128 GB RAM, featuring local SSD storage, optimized for single-core CPU tasks and small LLM inference.
c150: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 offers dedicated AMD EPYC Milan CPUs with 16 physical cores, 128 GB RAM, and 600 GB local SSD for demanding workloads.
c200: The Hetzner Cloud ccx53 provides dedicated x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan CPUs (16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs), 128 GB RAM, and 600 GB local SSD. It excels in single-core CPU performance and small LLM text generation.
w150: Hetzner Cloud's ccx53 is a dedicated CPU instance, leveraging an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz. It is provisioned with 128 GB of memory and includes 600 GB of local SSD storage. Performance metrics indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, achieving 3066.19 in stress-ng. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in bzip3 single-threaded decompression (13.79 GB/sec) and Passmark CPU encryption (39134.55). While multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are categorized as average, the instance shows top-tier speed for small (135M) LLM text generation at 360.07 tokens/sec. However, large (70B) LLM text generation performance is poor at 2.36 tokens/sec. This server is suitable for single-threaded CPU-bound applications, specific data processing tasks requiring strong compression/encryption, and small-scale LLM inference.
